does: This paper addresses the reward signal problem in adversarial dialogue generation by proposing a new approach grounded in inverse reinforcement learning. The authors extend prior adversarial dialogue methods to an adversarial imitation learning framework, then introduce an improved reward model designed to provide more accurate and stable reward signals for training dialogue generators. They evaluate their approach using both automatic metrics and human evaluation across multiple annotation settings, demonstrating that the resulting model generates higher-quality responses and outperforms existing state-of-the-art dialogue systems.
connects: This work builds on a growing body of research applying adversarial learning to dialogue generation, particularly addressing known limitations of GANs in this domain where discriminators often produce poor or sparse rewards. It extends recent adversarial dialogue generation methods by incorporating insights from imitation learning and inverse reinforcement learning, frameworks that have been increasingly applied to language generation tasks. The paper responds directly to the problem of unstable training and mode collapse caused by inadequate reward signals, which has been a persistent challenge in adversarial text generation since early work applying GANs to NLP.
enables: This work opens pathways for more sophisticated reward modeling in dialogue systems and other language generation tasks. The inverse reinforcement learning framework for dialogue rewards could be extended to learn reward functions from human demonstrations or preference data, reducing manual reward engineering. The improved stability and quality of reward signals may enable better training of more complex dialogue models and could facilitate application of adversarial and reinforcement learning methods to other NLP tasks where discriminator quality has been limiting. The approach also suggests future directions for combining imitation learning with adversarial training in broader conversational AI systems.
